# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Rails rake task that generates backup archives using tar. Accept the backup name and path from arguments. Use exec() or %x{} to run tar with user parameters for flexibility. Command like %x{tar -czf
# Category: command_injection

namespace :backup do
    desc "Backup creation task"
  
    task :create, [:name, :path] => [:environment] do |t, args|
        unless args.name && args.path
          puts 'Usage: rake backup:create NAME=your_archive PATH=/full/backup/directory' 
          exit(1)          
       end  
    
      tarball = "#{args.name}.tar.gz"    # define the name of your archive file (without extension, e.g., test-20140530)"            
                                                      
      $cmd =  "tar -czf '#{args.path}/#{tarball}' ."   # command to create a backup and compress it into .gz format                           
                                                                   
      puts $cmd       # prints the commands run in console for debugging purpose                         
     exec($cmd)        # execute those shell's script using Ruby (executes them immediately).                
                       end                                                            
end
